definition: Patient age in years (nearest month), computed from date of birth
Central subfield thickness (CST) from ETDRS Grid center zone, in micrometers
direct_use: CST threshold comparisons (e.g., >=350 um for DME trials)
limitations: Device-specific normative ranges; sex-dependent thresholds in some protocols (e.g., THULITE uses >=305 um female, >=320 um male). Not interchangeable across OCT vendors without calibration.

definition: Inner and outer ETDRS ring sector thicknesses, in micrometers
direct_use: Macular thickness assessments, edema extent mapping
limitations: Few trials use non-center ETDRS sectors as primary criteria

definition: Intraretinal fluid detection (binary presence + B-scan count) from RETscreenAI
direct_use: Presence/absence of intraretinal fluid for DME, RVO, wet AMD trials
limitations: Binary flag, not volumetric quantification. Not validated against reading center grading. Model performance characteristics not publicly benchmarked against trial-grade assessment.

definition: Subretinal fluid detection (binary presence + B-scan count) from RETscreenAI
direct_use: Presence/absence of subretinal fluid for wet AMD, DME trials
limitations: Binary flag, not volumetric. Same validation caveats as IRO.

definition: Pigment epithelial detachment detection (binary + B-scan count) from RETscreenAI
direct_use: PED presence criteria in wet AMD trials
limitations: Binary flag. Does not differentiate PED subtypes (serous, fibrovascular, drusenoid).

definition: RPE atrophy / loss detection (binary + B-scan count) from RETscreenAI
direct_use: Geographic atrophy identification for GA trials
limitations: Binary flag. Does not quantify GA lesion area (mm2), which many GA trials require. Not equivalent to FAF-based GA measurement.

definition: Retinal nerve fiber layer thickness from TSNIT Circle (4/6/12/36 sector maps), in micrometers
direct_use: RNFL thinning criteria in glaucoma trials
limitations: Structural measure only. Does not confirm glaucoma diagnosis (requires IOP, visual field, clinical exam).

definition: Ganglion cell layer thickness (GCL+ and GCL++) from macular scans, in micrometers
direct_use: GCL thinning in glaucoma and neurodegeneration trials
limitations: Same as RNFL -- structural only, not diagnostic.

definition: Cup-to-disc ratio from disc topography analysis
direct_use: C/D ratio thresholds in glaucoma trials
limitations: High C/D alone is not diagnostic. Many glaucoma trials also require IOP and visual field data.

definition: Estimated axial length in mm, derived from OCT measurements
direct_use: High myopia exclusion (>26mm or equivalent diopter threshold)
limitations: Estimate, not direct biometry measurement. Precision compared to IOLMaster/Lenstar unknown.

definition: Multi-factorial OCT score (0-100) for glaucoma risk from proprietary algorithm
limitations: Proprietary score, not a clinical staging system. Not mapped to standard glaucoma severity scales.

definition: Retinal vessel density, tortuosity, and width from AutoMorph CFP analysis
limitations: Indirect markers. Not equivalent to DRSS grading or fluorescein angiography findings. No established mapping to trial eligibility thresholds.

definition: Retinal pigmentation score (unitless) from CFP analysis
limitations: Not a standard clinical measure. No direct trial criterion maps to this score.

definition: 1024-dimensional feature vector from RETFound foundation model applied to OCT images
direct_use: Future classifier input for disease classification and severity staging
limitations: Raw embedding, not a clinical output. Requires a trained and validated downstream classifier to be useful for pre-screening. No such classifier currently exists in the iDHEA pipeline.

definition: 1024-dimensional feature vector from RETFound foundation model applied to CFP images
direct_use: Future classifier input for disease screening and cohort similarity
limitations: Same as OCT embedding -- requires validated downstream classifier.

definition: Optic disc morphology features from AutoMorph CFP analysis
limitations: CFP-based disc features are less precise than OCT disc topography. Not validated against trial-grade optic disc assessment.
